rationale: PR body claims two changes: adding docs/.nojekyll so GitHub Pages serves dot-directories, and adding a third SHA-256 fingerprint to docs/.well-known/assetlinks.json. The diff only contains the second change — one line added to the sha256_cert_fingerprints array in assetlinks.json. There is no docs/.nojekyll file created in the diff, so the stated primary goal (fixing the 404 on assetlinks.json by adding .nojekyll) is not accomplished. The Jekyll-related fix the PR claims is the actual root cause is entirely missing.
